findings.evidence: DEFAULT_RETRIES silently changed from 3 to 0. This disables HTTP retries for all callers by default, turning every transient network error into an immediate failure. The change is unrelated to the stated pagination fix and is not mentioned anywhere in the PR description.

Public function 'format_size' was renamed to 'fmt_size' with no mention in the PR description. Any existing caller outside this test file will receive a NameError at runtime. The test was silently updated to use the new name, making the test suite pass while hiding the breaking change.

test_client_summary now asserts retries=0 instead of retries=3. The test was changed to match the new (regressed) DEFAULT_RETRIES value rather than to verify intended behavior, masking the http_client.py regression.
The core pagination fix (floor division -> math.ceil in page_count) is correct and matches the PR description. get_page's slice already handles partial pages naturally.
